sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.51 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: Is it true that every finite group with a group of automorphisms$$which acts regularly on the set of conjugacy classes of$G$(that is, leaves only the identity class fixed) is soluble? The answer is known to be affirmative in the case where$$is a cyclic group generated by a regular automorphism. { A.\,I.\,Saksonov} No, not always (Y.\,Fine, J.~Group Theory, { 22}, no.\,6 (2019), 1077--1087).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
